nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Duston
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English:
habitational name for someone from Duston in Northamptonshire, named in Old English with dus ‘mound’ or dūst ‘dust’ + tūn ‘settlement, farmstead’.

perhaps a shortened form of Dustinton, from Distington in Cumberland, recorded as Distingtona and Dustinton in the early 13th century, named from an obscure first element (possibly a Old English personal name) + tūn ‘settlement, farmstead’.

variants.name: Dustin
meanings: English: variant of   Duston  .
Americanized form of French Canadian   Dostie  .
History: Many of the American bearers of the surname Dustin are descendants of Pierre de Bellot dit Dostie from France (see Dostie ).

variants.name: Dunstan
meanings: English:
from a Middle English personal name Dunstan (Old English Dūnstān), from dūn ‘hill’ + stān ‘stone’. This name was borne by a 10th-century archbishop of Canterbury who was later canonized.

habitational name from Dunston (Derbyshire, Lincolnshire, Norfolk), from the Old English personal name Dunn + tūn ‘settlement’. Occasionally the name may also arise from Dunstan (Northumberland), from Old English dūn ‘hill’ + stān ‘stone’.

variants.name: Distin
meanings: English (Devon): variant of Diston, itself a variant of Duston with unrounding of the vowel.

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isobel
meanings: Variant spelling of   Isabel .

en.lifeSummary: Isabel de Duston was born in 1059, in Dunmow, Essex, England, United Kingdom. She married Richard De Guerres before 1090, in Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons.

fr.lifeSummary: Isabel de Duston est née en 1059, à Dunmow, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle a épousé Richard De Guerres avant 1090, à Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Isabel de Duston wurde 1059 in Dunmow, Essex, England, United Kingdom geboren. Sie hat vor 1090 in Essex, England Richard De Guerres geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne.

it.lifeSummary: Isabel de Duston è nata il nel 1059 nel Dunmow, Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lei sposato Richard De Guerres prima del 1090, nel Essex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli.

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel de Duston(은)는 1059년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 던모에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1090년 이전 영국 에세스에서 Richard De Guerres(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Isabel de Duston nasceu em 1059, em Dunmow, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ela casou-se com Richard De Guerres antes 1090, em Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos.

ru.lifeSummary: Isabel de Duston родилась в 1059 г. в местности дунмоу, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard De Guerres перед 1090 г. в местности эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына.

es.lifeSummary: Isabel de Duston nació en 1059, en Dunmow, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Se casó con Richard De Guerres antes de 1090, en Essex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos.
